mentions.note: Weakness tied more to the Kenvue acquisition overhang than the core business; intriguing at 13x earnings with a 5% yield.
Ultimate consumer staples company; essential products; solid dividend holding.
Ultimate soft goods company; consumer staple with yield; defensive diversification.
Reports Tuesday morning; expect better quarter than P&G; merging with Kleenex in cash-stock deal but warming up to stock after P&G and Clorox rallies.
Defensive diversification option if unwilling to exit overvalued tech and speculators; steady dividend and brand strength.
5.25% yield, 13x earnings; recent six-alarm distributor fire temporary headwind; CEO Mike Shoe solid; Cramer advocates averaging down despite near-term challenges.

Suffering from massive fire that wiped out Scott toilet paper production but about to merge with Capital View in fantastic deal expected to produce tremendous returns; merger benefits not yet reflected in stock price.
Premier staple (Kleenex, paper towels, diapers), 13x earnings, 5.25% safe yield. Merging with Kenvue; CEO Michael Sue executing synergies. Defensive anchor as growth stocks stumble.
